_meta.title: Nekozoku Shōgundō — Lexicon
_meta.note: Single source of truth for the site glossary and the inline term popups. Reader-facing: no endgame/confidential material. Edit a definition here and it updates both the glossary page and every popup.

terms.def: The smallest counted instant of time — the base pulse of the clock.
A short moment; several ticks. The span of a held breath.
A brief while; several beats together. Long enough to cross a room, short enough to wait through.
The everyday day-cycle: one full rotation of the world, about thirty-two hours. The unit life is lived in.
A long stretch of many turns — a season-scale span. "The better part of an arc" is a considerable silence.
A great span of arcs; a long chapter of a life, or of a campaign.
The largest everyday unit of time — roughly one and a third of the long-forgotten Earth-years.
An epoch of five thousand ages; the grand division by which deep history is measured.

The ordinary faster-than-light gateway, anchored at a system's point of gravitational balance. The Fold-Gates are the roads that bind the stars together; when one falls silent, the system behind it goes dark.
The web of Fold-Gates linking the inhabited systems. Travel that leaves a record runs on the roads.
A system whose Fold-Gate has gone silent. Cut off from the roads, it can be reached only by those few who can open a Rip.
A tear opened straight through the dimensions, bypassing the roads entirely. A single weapon can carry a single person through at no cost.

A Rip held open by two or more dimensional weapons together — wide enough to move mass and cargo. It leaves the weapons that opened it dormant for a long while afterward, and leaves no record on the roads.
The ancient force beneath the ordinary world, older than memory — the substance the Ascended and the Weapons of Creation are made to wield.
The dimensional structure underlying ordinary space — what those attuned to it perceive in place of an ordinary sense.
Not a weapon but a method: the means by which powers from beyond reach into and inhabit crude vessel-weapons.
Beings who, long ago, rose out of ordinary existence into the dimensions themselves. Some bound their power into weapons; not all could.
The great dimensional weapons, each an Ascended given form, each borne by a single rare host. Spoken of as the Nine.
The Weapons of Creation of the light side — nine in number.
The dark counterpart to the Nine. That the balance stands at thirteen against nine is a weight the light has always carried.
A lesser, cruder weapon beneath the great ones — a hollow thing inhabited from beyond by way of dark energy.

terms.lit: a host of a dimensional weapon
terms.def: One of the rare few bonded to a dimensional weapon. Only a bearer can open a Rip — and so only a bearer can reach a dark system. There are perhaps a dozen in the whole galaxy.
Byakko's paired weapon — the one that is all things and its negative image that is none.
Yamaneko's weapon, holding the poles of Heaven and Earth.
Yuki's weapon, holding the poles of Flora and Fauna — all that grows and all that moves.
Arashi's weapon — the wind. A double-edged blade in the hands of the grandmaster.
Mine's bow. The arrow does not travel to its mark — it simply arrives there.

Kika's weapon — and the one weapon among the Nine that is no object at all. She is one with her Ascended so wholly that it lives in her body rather than in any blade: an empty-handed yielding-art that turns a force back down the line it came.

terms.lit: Japanese 溶岩刀, "lava blade"; the Spirit
terms.def: Iwa's great lava-sword. It does not cut. It ends — and its fissures are not cracks in matter but tears in the dimension behind it.
The cat-derived people — warriors and rulers, the house of the Shōgunate. The empire takes its name from them.
A person of the Nekozoku. The rarest among them are born with two tails — a mutation legend-rare; three of one age, unprecedented.
The scholars and scientists of the galaxy — keepers of learning, libraries, and the dimensional record.
The agrarian people — growers and keepers of the supply network that feeds the settled worlds.

terms.lit: the Azure Dragon of the East
terms.def: The dragon-aspected people of the East; one of the great civilizations beyond the Shōgunate.
The wolf-derived people, bound to the Shōgunate by the Armistice. Their envoys keep its terms — and its silences.
A solitary, self-possessed people — born to teeth and quickness, but never made to run in any pack.

A fox people of the Frontier's green-cluster worlds, born to a caste marked by the number of their tails. Known for foresight and chosen neutrality, and said to be always a step ahead. Their homeworlds drink so deep of dimensional energy that the very stars there burn an impossible pale green.
The bear-derived people; among the few the Kitsune count as close allies.
A creed of subsumption rather than a bloodline — the faction gathered under Rex, that would dissolve the self into the whole.
The adversary of a war now passing into legend. Byakko carries the epithet Slayer of Fahng.
The supreme general-ruler of the Nekozoku — the title at the heart of the Shōgunate.

terms.lit: Japanese 将軍道, "the way of the Shogun"
terms.def: The discipline and creed by which the Nekozoku rule — and the name of this work.
The title borne by a recognized scholar. (As in Hakase Ren, the librarian of Oumura.)
A healer's honorific. (As in Isha Riki.)
A physician's title among certain peoples. (As in Vrach Roak.)
The steward of a great house. An ancient loanword worn down over the ages until "majo" — witch — began to show through the middle of it. (Kit holds the office of the Royal House.)
A high office of the Usagizoku court; the Third Reagent stands near the Emperor's hand.
A senior officer of state finance. (As in Banking Commissioner Feelon.)
The foremost of a pack — Rex's title among the DarkPack.
The world at the heart of the Shōgunate. Its sun rises in the west and sets in the east — the world turns against the old habit of memory.
The twelve-world system around the violet sun, girdled by two vast ring-megastructures and threaded with moons.
Not a spiral but a ring: an ancient, dim, embered circle of stars around a dark hollow, with a single blazing Core at its dead center. The whole sky of this age.
The ultramassive black hole at the exact center of the Galaxy's void — blazing now because it feeds on the dust of its own last outburst.
The thinly-watched edge of settled space, where the roads run sparse — homeworlds of the Kitsune, the Lycan, the Ursa, and others who keep their distance.
A frontier waystation, scene of a theft carried out off the roads and without a trace.
A system gone dark — and the place where the hand walking the frontier first grew careless enough to be caught.
A system gone dark; once Usagizoku research territory, its library of dimensional scholarship carried off rather than burned.
A system whose Fold-Gate failed long ages ago — dark so long it has passed out of all but the oldest reckoning.
The treaty governing dealings with the Lycan Pack. Certain works, teams, and movements are prohibited under its terms — and the prohibitions can be used as cover by those who know them.
The crest a person is given at birth — a mark of house, origin, and the place one is owed back to.

terms.lit: the Four Guardians of the directions
terms.def: The four great aspects of the compass: Byakko of the West, Seiryū of the East, Suzaku of the South, Genbu of the North — guardians, directions, and civilizations at once.
